content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč which contains pairwise distinct elements and a sequence B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč, which also contains pairwise distinct elements (but not necessarily distinct from elements of AAA). For each valid iii, 1‚Č§Ai,Bi‚Č§2‚čÖN1 \leq A_i, B_i \leq 2 \cdot N1‚Č§Ai‚Äč,Bi‚Äč‚Č§2‚čÖN.

You may rotate BBB as many times as you want. A rotation consists of moving the first element of the sequence to the end. Afterwards, let's define a sequence C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äč,C2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äč as Ci=(Ai+Bi)%NC_i = (A_i + B_i) \% NCi‚Äč=(Ai‚Äč+Bi‚Äč)%N for each valid iii.

There are NNN possible sequences CCC. Find the lexicographically smallest of these sequences.

**Note:** A sequence xxx is lexicographically smaller than a different sequence yyy if xi‚ÄČ<‚ÄČyix_i‚ÄČ<‚ÄČy_ixi‚Äč‚ÄČ<‚ÄČyi‚Äč, where iii is the smallest valid index where the sequences xxx and yyy differ.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äč,C2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äč denoting the lexicographically smallest sequence.
constraints: 1‚Č§Ai,Bi‚Č§2‚čÖN1 \leq A_i, B_i \leq 2 \cdot N1‚Č§Ai‚Äč,Bi‚Äč‚Č§2‚čÖN for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: After rotating BBB once, it becomes (3,4,1)(3,4,1)(3,4,1). Now C=(1,2,0)C = (1,2,0)C=(1,2,0). This is the lexicographically smallest of all possible sequences CCC.
